entry.summary: The experiences and struggles of George Myers, more commonly known under his pen name of Joseph Edwards, or as Fundi, the Carribean Situationist. In text and audio format.

entry.title: Poland 1980-1982: Class struggle and the crisis of capital - Henri Simon
entry.summary: Henri Simon's brilliant and detailed account and analysis of the militant working class struggles in Poland from 1980 to 1982 which were a major contributing factor to the downfall of the USSR.

entry.title: States of emergency: cultures of revolt in Italy from 1968 to 1978 - Robert Lumley
entry.summary: The complete text of the definitive book on the mass social movements in Italy in the 1960s and 70s in which author Robert Lumley traces their development, growth then recuperation and decline. Highly recommended.

entry.title: Strike! - Jeremy Brecher
entry.summary: In possibly the best book on the American working class movement ever written, Jeremy Brecher narrates the hidden history of mass strikes from 1877 to 1970 from the point of view of the workers themselves.

entry.title: The Evolution of Local Soviets in Petrograd - Alexander Rabinowich
entry.summary: The Evolution of Local Soviets in Petrograd, November 1917 to June 1918: The Case of the First City District Soviet Alexander Rabinowitch

entry.title: The Japanese Labour Movement - Towards Total Dissolution?
entry.summary: The Japanese Labour Movement - Towards Total Dissolution? Watanabe Ben

entry.title: The Jungle
entry.summary: The Jungle

entry.title: The Lordstown struggle and the real crisis in production - Ken Weller
entry.summary: Fascinating pamphlet by Solidarity on the informal workers struggle against the frenetic pace of work at a General Motors plant, and the later co-optation of the struggle by the auto workers union.

entry.title: The Voice of the People newspaper
entry.summary: Voice of the People was the new name of the Lumberjack the Wobbly Weekly covering New Orleans and the surrounding area.

entry.title: The Workers' Opposition - Alexandra Kollontai
entry.summary: Kollontai's pamphlet was one of the central theoretical works of the Workers' Opposition movement within the Bolshevik Party, arguing for increased union control of the economy and the debureaucratisation of the party hierarchy.

entry.title: ﻿Trade unionism or socialism: the revolt against work - John Zerzan
entry.summary: 'Trade Unionism or Socialism - the revolt against work' was published by Solidarity in 1976. (Solidarity pamphlet No. 47).

entry.title: Weakening the dam - Twin Cities IWW
entry.summary: A pamphlet put out by the Twin Cities IWW branch for the purpose of promoting the development of workplace organisers, based on their experiences of organising at work. It offers the sort of practical advice we could all be implementing in our own workplaces.

entry.title: Wildcat journal (UK)
entry.summary: Online archive of the newsletter and journal of the UK communist group Wildcat , published in the 1980s and 1990s.

entry.title: Worker-student action committees, France May '68 - Roger Gregoire and Fredy Perlman
entry.summary: An in-depth look at the worker-student action committees in the uprising of France May '68.

entry.title: Workers against work: labor in Paris and Barcelona during the Popular Fronts - Michael Seidman
entry.summary: Why did a revolution occur in Spain and not in France in 1936?

entry.title: Workers' councils and the economics of self-managed society - Cornelius Castoriadis
entry.summary: Cornelius Castoriadis/Paul Cardan's proposals for the workings of a society based on the principle of self-management by workers' councils, originally published in English by Solidarity in 1972. We have significant disagreements with it as it retains the key features of capitalism, but we reproduce it for reference.

entry.title: 1960s: Beyond the New Left Part 2- Muto Ichiyo & Inoue Reiko
entry.summary: Beyond the New Left Part 2- Muto Ichiyo & Inoue Reiko

entry.title: A worker's critique of parecon

category.term: revolutionary union Industrial Workers of the World

entry.summary: Criticism of the idea of participatory economics, or parecon, from the perspective of a worker. Despite its theoreticians' grand plans, we resist work now and we would continue to do so under parecon, Steven argues. Michael Albert subsequently responds.

entry.title: A world without money: communism - Les Amis de 4 Millions de Jeunes Travailleurs

category.term: Les amis de 4 millions de jeunes travailleurs

entry.summary: Un Monde Sans Argent: Le Communisme was originally published in three parts, as three separate pamphlets, in France, between 1975-6. It was produced by Dominique Blanc, shortly after the dissolution of the Organisation des Jeunes Travailleurs révolutionnaires . entry.summary: Beyond the New Left Part 2 - Muto Ichiyo and Inoue Reiko Presented in PDF format. (1mb)

entry.title: Class composition and the theory of the party at the origins of the workers' council movement
entry.summary: Sergio Bologna on the composition of the working class and the role of the revolutionary party in the German movement for workers' councils at the start of the 20th century.

entry.title: Class War on the home front - revolutionary opposition to the Second World War: the Anti-Parliamentary Communist Federation
entry.summary: Wildcat's excellent pamphlet on the Anti-Parliamentary Communist Federation in the first half of the 20th century, and its contributions to debates around the Spanish civil war, World War II and the role of revolutionary organisation.

entry.title: Community and Communism in Russia - Jacques Camatte
entry.summary: This text was originally intended to be the introduction to a french language edition of two of Amadeo Bordiga's texts on Russia : Russia and Revolution in Marxist Theory and Economic and Social Structure of Russia Today.

entry.title: Eclipse and re-emergence of the communist movement - Gilles Dauvé and François Martin
entry.summary: A book by François Martin and "Jean Barrot" (aka Gilles Dauvé), influential since the 1970s among the English-speaking ultra-left. The themes include the refusal of work, the legacy of left communism/the ultra-left, and a restatement of revolution, as not merely a singular event, but the unleashing of a dynamic – 'communisation'.

entry.title: Endnotes journal
entry.summary: Partial online archive of Endnotes, an irregular communist theoretical journal produced by a discussion group of the same name based in Britain and the US.

entry.title: Essays on Marx's theory of value - Isaak Rubin
entry.summary: Essays on Marx's theories of value by a seminal Marxist writer. Originally published in the former Soviet Union, Rubin's work was suppressed and after 1928 it was never re-issued. Translation by Miloš Samardžija and Fredy Perlman.

entry.title: Fundamental principles of communist production and distribution
entry.summary: Full text of Fundamental Principles of Communist Production and Distribution, Collective work of the Group of International Communists of Holland (GIK), 1930

entry.title: How to overthrow the Illuminati
entry.summary: This pamphlet gives an excellent account, analysis and debunking of conspiracy theories and proposes a practical alternative for poor, black and working class people for emancipation.

entry.title: Hunting Specters: A Political History of the Purges in the Communist Party of the Philippines
entry.summary: A historical study of the violent purges committed by the Communist Party of the Philippines and their armed component the New People's Army.

entry.title: Karl Marx: man and fighter - Boris Nicolaievsky and Otto Maenchen-Helfen
entry.summary: Part of the series of biographies of Karl Marx . 1933 biography of Karl Marx by Otto Maenchen-Helfen and Boris Ivanovich Nicolaevsky (1887-1966), who was a revolutionary Russian Marxist activist, archivist, and historian. Nicolaevsky is best remembered as one of the leading Menshevik public intellectuals of the 20th Century.

entry.title: Left-Wing Lies Ken Ellis
entry.summary: 6th edition (2003) What a Worker Found in his Quest for a Definition of Socialism

entry.title: Marx and Engels collected works
entry.summary: A collection of writings from Marx and Engels, consisting of 50 volumes, known as the Marx-Engels Collected Works (MECW). The collection includes all of Marx's and Engels' major works, as well as previously unpublished or lesser-known texts (e.g. letters, newspaper articles, manuscripts and so on). However, the collection does not include all of Marx's and Engels' writings (such as Marx's and Enge

entry.title: Open letter to comrade Lenin - Herman Gorter
entry.summary: Gorter responds with radical criticism to the reformist programme advocated by Lenin - i.e., for European communists to work within their Parliaments and existing unions.

entry.title: Out of the Ghetto - Joe Jacobs
entry.summary: "My Youth In The East End: Communism and Fascism 1913-1939." Essential autobiograpy of a working class Jewish activist in London.

entry.title: Outline study course in Marxian economics - Paul Mattick
entry.summary: Paul Mattick's guide to Capital, created for study groups for unemployed workers for classes on the first volume of Marx's Capital ", first published as a pamphlet by International Council Correspondence in 1935.

entry.title: Peasants into Russians: The Utopian Essence of War Communism - Bertrand M Patenaude
entry.summary: Peasants into Russians - The Utopian Essence of War Communism Bertrand M Patenaude

entry.title: Reading capital politically - Harry Cleaver
entry.summary: Harry Cleaver's seminal work on forming a practical, political interpretation of Marx's Capital.

entry.title: Society of the Spectacle - Guy Debord
entry.summary: French Situationist Guy Debord's seminal analysis of consumer capitalism in the late 20th century. Translated by Ken Knabb, 2002.

entry.title: Storming heaven: class composition and struggle in Italian Autonomist Marxism - Steve Wright
entry.summary: Book documenting the historical context of the Italian operaista tradition, and the development of the category of class composition.

entry.title: The civil war in France - Karl Marx
entry.summary: Karl Marx's contemporary account of the Paris Commune, placing it in context fo the wider events in France at the time. The Paris Commune significantly changed Marx's ideas about the "Dictatorship of the Proletariat", and his support for its organisation structures suggests a different trajectory of revolutionary organisation to the "Marxist" revolutions in the 20th Century.

entry.title: The communist left in Germany 1918-1921 - Gilles Dauvé and Denis Authier
entry.summary: A analysis of the revolutionary movements in Europe at the end of World War I, their contradictions and limitations.

entry.title: The communist manifesto - Karl Marx and Frederick Engels
entry.summary: The Manifesto of the Communist Party Communist Manifesto was commissioned by the Communist League and published in 1848, and remains one of the world's most influential political tracts.

entry.title: The Communist Manifesto
entry.summary: The Communist Manifesto

entry.title: The factors of race and nation in Marxist theory - Amadeo Bordiga

entry.title: The holy family, or critique of critical criticism - Karl Marx and Frederick Engels
entry.summary: Marx and Engels' 1844 critique of the Young Hegelians and their ideas which were very popular in academia at the time.

entry.title: The incomplete Marx - Felton C. Shortall
entry.summary: Part of the series of biographies of Karl Marx . Libertarian Marxist Felton C. Shortall's detailed analysis of the revolutionary ideas of Karl Marx.

entry.title: The making of an Atlantic ruling class - Kees van der Pijl
entry.summary: Kees van der Pijl traces the formation of a transatlantic ruling class from the early 20th century through to the Cold War era.

entry.title: The Mandate of Heaven - Nigel Harris

entry.title: The origin of the family, private property and the state - Frederick Engels
entry.summary: Engels' 1884 book focusing on early human history, following the disintegration of the primitive community and the emergence of a class society based on private property. Engels looks into the origin and essence of the state, and concludes it is bound to wither away leaving a classless society.

entry.title: The power of women and the subversion of the community - Mariarosa Dalla Costa and Selma James
entry.summary: An influential pamphlet by Mariarosa Dalla Costa and Selma James in 1972 that used a feminist reading of Marx to challenge Left orthodoxy on the role of women, their labour and their struggles.

entry.title: The soul of man under socialism - Oscar Wilde
entry.summary: The famous writer and poet Oscar Wilde's key text outlining his personal vision for a libertarian socialist society, and its implications for personal freedom and potential.

entry.title: The State and Revolution
entry.summary: The State and Revolution

entry.title: The struggle against the state and other essays - Nestor Makhno
entry.summary: An anthology of articles by Ukrainian libertarian communist and guerrilla fighter Nestor Makhno

entry.title: Theses on the Chinese revolution - Cajo Brendel
entry.summary: Cajo Brendel discusses the Chinese revolution and conflicts between the Chinese Communist Party and the working class and peasantry, and conflicts within the Party itself.

entry.title: Catalyst - Solidarity Federation
entry.summary: Complete online archive of issues of Catalyst, the regular agitational newspaper of the UK anarcho-syndicalist organisation the Solidarity Federation.

entry.title: Mutual aid: a factor of evolution - Peter Kropotkin
entry.summary: Russian anarchist Peter Kropotkin's massively influential work on mutual aid and co-operation as a factor in evolution, written in 1902.
